action: Plugin-phase classify rules omit the markdown heading prefix, so hasSection (specrunner.go hasSection: HasPrefix(TrimSpace(line), section)) never matches the actual report headings. Verified: .evolve/phases/behavior-baseline/phase.json declares require_sections:["Baseline"] but the agent emits '## Baseline' -> deterministic FAIL on a correct artifact (cycles 249+250, 2/2; the runner then fail-opens the spine with 'WARN spine not satisfied'). behavior-compare/phase.json has the same latent bug (require_sections:["Comparison","Verdict"] vs '## Comparison'/'## Verdict' headings) and only PASSes by accident when prose lines like 'Verdict: PASS' start a line. THIRD victim confirmed: smell-scan/phase.json (require_sections:["Findings"] vs '## Findings') PASSed cycle 249 by accident, FAILed cycle 250 (smell-scan-usage.json verdict FAIL, 261s) on a correct artifact — the bug class is intermittent per phase per cycle, depending on whether any prose line happens to start with the bare rule string. Cycle 250 escalation: behavior-compare ALSO flipped to FAIL (512s, grep confirms 0 lines start with bare 'Comparison'/'Verdict') on an artifact whose own sentinel was a legitimate WARN — so the classifier verdict now masks the agent's real WARN signal, and 3 of 3 affected phases have produced wrong verdicts in a single cycle. Fix BOTH layers: (1) correct the two phase.json files to '## '-prefixed sections (config fix); (2) structural: make `evolve phases create` / phase-spec validation lint require_sections entries against heading-style matching, or make hasSection heading-aware (strip leading #+ whitespace before compare) so the whole class dies - choose one home, no dual matching semantics. Also consider: a FAIL verdict from an optional evaluate phase should not silently fail-open the spine WARN path 2 cycles in a row without surfacing a defect signal. Acceptance: behavior-baseline classifies PASS on its own correct artifact in a live cycle; a unit test pins heading-matching semantics; lint or loader rejects (or auto-normalizes) prefix-less heading rules.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/runs/cycle-249/behavior-baseline-usage.json + .evolve/runs/cycle-250/behavior-baseline-usage.json (verdict FAIL, attempt 1) vs '## Baseline' heading in cycle-250/behavior-baseline.md; specrunner.go hasSection; batch log 'WARN spine not satisfied' x4
